behavior: Release-blocking regressions in resume validation, narrow salary filters, first-run dark contrast, support-report testing, and desktop close-to-tray behavior are corrected and regression-protected.
revision_scope: Verified working tree based on the recorded revision.
changes: Deferred the required resume name error until field blur or an attempted step advance.
Kept both salary bounds inside the exact 360-pixel minimum viewport.
Restored readable first-run heading contrast in the dark app shell.
Made support-report privacy coverage deterministic without treating timestamp digits as leaked phone data.
Prevented main-window close from terminating the process and hid it for tray-based reopen.

commands.relevant_result: The exact-runtime source gate passed harness, security, dependency, SQLx, duplication, documentation, lint, 828 script tests, 2,928 frontend tests, production and component-library builds, 10 smoke journeys, and the complete standard Rust lane.
All 288 browser journeys passed across both configured engines in 123,411.608 ms with zero unexpected, flaky, or skipped outcomes.
commands.caveat: The enforced budget was 240,000 ms and 320 tests.

commands.relevant_result: The complete Rust workspace passed with every optional feature enabled.
commands.caveat: Tests explicitly classified as live-network, model-download, environment-isolation, manual-browser, or hanging stress checks remained ignored.

commands.relevant_result: Release metadata, policy, preflight, staged publication, package, checksum, SBOM, attestation, and public-verifier gates passed for 2.9.5.
commands.caveat: Public trust signing and notarization remain release-execution requirements.

commands.relevant_result: A local application bundle was built from the verified correction source.
commands.caveat: The debug bundle was used only for native lifecycle QA and was not retained as a release artifact.

commands.relevant_result: The isolated encrypted bundle launched with one visible window; close reduced visible windows to zero while the process stayed alive; tray reopen restored one visible window; tray quit stopped the process.
commands.caveat: The packaged desktop-control service did not attach, so native accessibility and compositor APIs supplied the direct observations.

commands.command: npm run harness:check && npm run lint:file-size && npm run lint:docs && npm run test:scripts && git diff --check
commands.relevant_result: Canonical state, file-size governance, maintained documentation, all 828 script tests, and diff hygiene passed after the final state update.
commands.caveat: Five changed files remain above the advisory modularization review threshold but below every hard line and byte limit.
